description: Implement all 93 Vant UI components with 100% feature parity on Vue Lynx. Each component must match Vant's props, events, slots, methods, CSS variables, and animations. Features unsupported on Lynx must be documented with reasons.

rules: For each component, FIRST fetch Vant source from GitHub: curl the .tsx/.vue, types.ts, and .less files from https://raw.githubusercontent.com/youzan/vant/main/packages/vant/src/{component}/
Compare our implementation line-by-line against Vant source code
Every Vant prop must be implemented or documented as unsupported with a specific reason
Every Vant event must be implemented with correct timing (opened/closed AFTER animation)
Every Vant slot must be implemented with correct slot props
CSS variables must match Vant naming: --van-{component}-{property}
Animations must use Vue <Transition> component with proper enter/leave CSS classes
Use lynxbase skill to query Lynx APIs when implementing platform-specific features
Browser verify: compare with Vant demo at https://vant-ui.github.io/vant/#/en-US/{component}
Unsupported Lynx features (teleport, lockScroll, closeOnPopstate) must be documented in a comment at top of component
Props for unsupported features should still be defined for API compatibility
When done with a component, add a parity report comment at the top: Props X/Y, Events X/Y, Slots X/Y
lynxLimitations: teleport: Lynx has no DOM, Vue Teleport not supported
lockScroll: Lynx scrolling mechanism differs from browser, no equivalent API
closeOnPopstate: Lynx has no browser history API
tag prop (render as different HTML element): Lynx uses view/text elements only
native-type (form submit): Lynx has no native form submission
CSS pseudo-elements (::before/::after): limited support in Lynx
CSS @keyframes: may need Lynx animation API alternative
SVG: Lynx has limited SVG support, may need canvas/image alternatives
IntersectionObserver: query lynxbase for Lynx equivalent
getBoundingClientRect: query lynxbase for Lynx equivalent

userStories.title: Implement Icon with full Vant parity
userStories.description: Port the Vant Icon component to Vue Lynx with full feature parity. All props, events, slots, and methods must match Vant's API.
acceptanceCriteria: Props: name (string, default: '') — icon name from the Vant icon set or a full image URL; dot (boolean, default: false) — whether to show a small red dot in the upper right corner; badge (number | string, default: '') — badge content shown in the upper right corner; badge-props (BadgeProps, default: none) — additional props passed to the internal Badge component; color (string, default: 'inherit') — icon color; size (number | string, default: 'inherit') — icon size, accepts px values or rem strings; class-prefix (string, default: 'van-icon') — CSS class name prefix for the icon font; tag (string, default: 'i') — HTML tag of the root element
Events: click (MouseEvent) — emitted when the icon is clicked
Slots: none defined in the API
Methods: none exposed via ref

Lynx adaptations: font-based icon rendering via iconfont is unavailable in Lynx; icon rendering must use image assets or SVG equivalents; the tag prop cannot map to arbitrary HTML tags in Lynx; badge overlay positioning must use Lynx absolute layout; URL-based icons should use Lynx image element
Demo page path: packages/vant-lynx/src/demo/icon/index.vue
userStories.notes: Full Vant parity achieved with Lynx adaptations documented

userStories.title: Implement Search with full Vant parity
userStories.description: Port the Vant Search component to Vue Lynx with full feature parity. All props, events, slots, and methods must match Vant's API.
acceptanceCriteria: Props: v-model (number|string, no default) — current input value; label (string, no default) — text label shown to the left of the input field; name (string, no default) — form field identifier used on form submit; shape (string, default 'square', options: 'square' | 'round') — shape of the input container; id (string, default 'van-search-n-input') — id attribute of the input element, also sets the label's for attribute; background (string, default '#f2f2f2') — background color of the entire search bar; maxlength (number|string, no default) — maximum character length of the input; placeholder (string, no default) — placeholder text; clearable (boolean, default true) — whether to show a clear button; clear-icon (string, default 'clear') — icon name used for the clear button; clear-trigger (string, default 'focus', options: 'focus' | 'always') — when to display clear icon: 'focus' shows only when focused, 'always' shows whenever value is non-empty; autofocus (boolean, default false) — auto-focuses input on mount, unsupported on iOS; show-action (boolean, default false) — whether to show the right action button; action-text (string, default 'Cancel') — text of the default action button; disabled (boolean, default false) — disables the input field; readonly (boolean, default false) — makes the input read-only; error (boolean, default false) — highlights input content in red; error-message (string, no default) — error message shown below the input; formatter ((val: string) => string, no default) — function to format input value; format-trigger (string, default 'onChange', options: 'onChange' | 'onBlur') — when to apply the formatter; input-align (string, default 'left', options: 'left' | 'center' | 'right') — text alignment within the input; left-icon (string, default 'search') — icon name shown inside the left of the input; right-icon (string, no default) — icon name shown inside the right of the input; autocomplete (string, no default) — native autocomplete attribute for the input element

Events: search — emitted when user presses the search/enter key, argument is value: string; update:model-value — emitted on every input change, argument is value: string; focus — emitted when input is focused, argument is event: Event; blur — emitted when input loses focus, argument is event: Event; click-input — emitted when the input area is clicked, argument is event: MouseEvent; click-left-icon — emitted when the left icon is clicked, argument is event: MouseEvent; click-right-icon — emitted when the right icon is clicked, argument is event: MouseEvent; clear — emitted when the clear icon is clicked, argument is event: MouseEvent; cancel — emitted when the default cancel button is clicked, no argument

Methods exposed via ref: focus() — programmatically focuses the input; blur() — programmatically blurs the input

Lynx adaptations: native HTML form/input element replaced with Lynx input component; keyboard search action (inputmode/enterkeyhint) must use Lynx input props; autocomplete attribute not applicable in Lynx; autofocus behavior via Lynx input focus API; MouseEvent arguments replaced with Lynx event equivalents
Demo page path: src/pages/search/index.vue

acceptanceCriteria: Props: v-model (string, current value); show (boolean, whether to show keyboard); title (string, keyboard title, default undefined); theme (string, default 'default', can be set to 'custom'); maxlength (number|string, default Infinity); transition (boolean, default true, whether to show transition animation); z-index (number|string, default 100); extra-key (string|string[], default '', content of bottom-left key; pass array of two strings for multiple extra keys in custom theme); close-button-text (string, default undefined, text of close button); delete-button-text (string, default undefined, shows delete icon if not set); close-button-loading (boolean, default false, show loading on close button in custom theme); show-delete-key (boolean, default true); blur-on-close (boolean, default true, emit blur when close clicked); hide-on-click-outside (boolean, default true); teleport (string|Element, default undefined); safe-area-inset-bottom (boolean, default true); random-key-order (boolean, default false)

Events: input — emitted when a key is pressed, argument: key: string; delete — emitted when delete key is pressed, no arguments; close — emitted when close button is clicked, no arguments; blur — emitted when close button is clicked or keyboard is blurred, no arguments; show — emitted when keyboard is fully displayed, no arguments; hide — emitted when keyboard is fully hidden, no arguments
Slots: delete — custom delete key content; extra-key — custom extra key content; title-left — custom title left content

Lynx adaptations: keyboard must render as overlay panel (no native keyboard); touch events for key press (touchstart preferred over click for responsive feel); teleport not applicable in Lynx; safe-area-inset-bottom must use Lynx safe area APIs; hide-on-click-outside requires Lynx touch outside detection; transition animation via Lynx animation APIs
Demo page path: src/pages/number-keyboard/index.vue

userStories.title: Implement PasswordInput with full Vant parity

acceptanceCriteria: Props: value (string, default '', the password value to display); info (string, default undefined, bottom info/hint message); error-info (string, default undefined, bottom error message shown in red); length (number|string, default 6, max length / number of input cells); gutter (number|string, default 0, gap between input cells); mask (boolean, default true, whether to mask/hide the value with dots); focused (boolean, default false, whether to show the focused cursor blinking in the active cell)

userStories.title: Implement Uploader with full Vant parity
userStories.description: Port the Vant Uploader component to Vue Lynx with full feature parity. All props, events, slots, and methods must match Vant's API.
acceptanceCriteria: Props: v-model (FileListItem[], list of uploaded files, default: -); accept (string, accepted file types per HTML spec, default: 'image/*'); name (number|string, input name, default: -); preview-size (number|string|Array, size of preview image — Array sets [width,height] separately, default: '80px'); preview-image (boolean, whether to show image previews, default: true); preview-full-image (boolean, whether clicking a preview opens full-screen preview, default: true); preview-options (object, options passed to ImagePreview component, default: -); multiple (boolean, enable multiple file selection, default: false); disabled (boolean, disable the uploader, default: false); readonly (boolean, make upload area readonly, default: false); deletable (boolean, show delete icon on previews, default: true); reupload (boolean, v4.4.0, enable reupload mode — disables image preview, default: false); show-upload (boolean, show the upload trigger area, default: true); lazy-load (boolean, lazy load previews — requires Lazyload component, default: false); capture (string, capture attribute e.g. 'camera', default: -); after-read (Function, hook after file is read, default: -); before-read (Function, hook before reading — return false or rejected Promise to abort, default: -); before-delete (Function, hook before deletion — return false or rejected Promise to abort, default: -); max-size (number|string|(file: File)=>boolean, max file size in bytes, default: Infinity); max-count (number|string, max number of files, default: Infinity); result-type (string: 'file'|'text'|'dataUrl', type of FileReader result, default: 'dataUrl'); upload-text (string, text shown in upload area, default: -); image-fit (string, CSS object-fit for preview images, default: 'cover'); upload-icon (string, icon in upload area, default: 'photograph')

Events: oversize — emitted when a file exceeds max-size, argument: same structure as after-read callback; click-upload — emitted when upload area is clicked, argument: event: MouseEvent; click-preview — emitted when a preview image is clicked, argument: same structure as after-read callback; click-reupload — emitted when reupload image is clicked, argument: same structure as after-read callback; close-preview — emitted when full-screen preview is closed, no arguments; delete — emitted when a preview file is deleted, argument: same structure as after-read callback

Slots: default — custom upload trigger area (no slotProps); preview-delete — custom delete icon (no slotProps); preview-cover — custom overlay content on top of each preview image, slotProps: item: FileListItem

Methods (via ref): closeImagePreview() — closes the full-screen image preview, no args, no return; chooseFile() — programmatically triggers file chooser (must be called in user-action context), no args, no return; reuploadFile(index: number) — triggers file chooser for reupload, overwriting the file at given index (must be called in user-action context, v4.9.3), returns void

Lynx adaptations: native file input element (<input type=file>) does not exist in Lynx; file picking must use Lynx native API (e.g. lynx.chooseImage / lynx.chooseFile) instead; FileReader API for result-type dataUrl/text is not available in Lynx JS runtime — use Lynx native equivalents; full-screen image preview relies on ImagePreview component which itself needs Lynx adaptation; lazy-load integration with IntersectionObserver is not available in Lynx; accept and capture props are web-only attributes and must map to Lynx native picker type options; before-read receives a File object on web — Lynx equivalent will be a platform-specific file descriptor object; preview-full-image and preview-options are contingent on ImagePreview Lynx support
Demo page path: src/pages/uploader/index.vue

userStories.title: Implement Signature with full Vant parity

Slots: tips — custom tips content when Canvas unsupported

userStories.title: Implement ShareSheet with full Vant parity

acceptanceCriteria: Props: v-model:show (boolean, default false) — controls visibility of the share sheet; options (Option[] | Option[][], default []) — flat array for single row or array of arrays for multi-row share options; title (string, no default) — title text shown at the top of the panel; cancel-text (string, default 'Cancel') — text of the cancel button; description (string, no default) — description text shown below the title; duration (number|string, default 0.3) — transition animation duration in seconds; z-index (number|string, default 2000+) — z-index of the panel; round (boolean, default true) — whether the top corners are rounded; overlay (boolean, default true) — whether to show a background overlay; overlay-class (string|Array|object, no default) — custom CSS class(es) for the overlay; overlay-style (object, no default) — custom inline styles for the overlay; lock-scroll (boolean, default true) — whether to prevent background scrolling; lazy-render (boolean, default true) — whether to defer rendering until first appearance; close-on-popstate (boolean, default true) — closes the panel on browser popstate event; close-on-click-overlay (boolean, default true) — closes the panel when the overlay is clicked; safe-area-inset-bottom (boolean, default true) — adds bottom padding for devices with home indicator; teleport (string|Element, no default) — DOM target to mount the ShareSheet into; before-close ((action: string) => boolean | Promise<boolean>, no default) — async callback called before closing to allow cancellation

Option object shape: name (string) — display name of the option; description (string, optional) — sub-description under the name; icon (string) — built-in icon key ('wechat' | 'weibo' | 'qq' | 'link' | 'qrcode' | 'poster' | 'weapp-qrcode' | 'wechat-moments') or an image URL; className (string, optional) — extra CSS class applied to the option item

Events: select — emitted when an option is tapped, arguments are option: Option and index: number; cancel — emitted when the cancel button is tapped, no arguments; open — emitted when the panel starts opening; close — emitted when the panel starts closing; opened — emitted after the panel open animation completes; closed — emitted after the panel close animation completes; click-overlay — emitted when the overlay is clicked, argument is event: MouseEvent
Slots: title — custom title content; description — custom description content; cancel — custom cancel button content
Methods: none exposed via ref

Lynx adaptations: teleport/portal to document body is not applicable in Lynx; use Lynx overlay/modal system instead; lock-scroll requires Lynx scroll-lock API; animation uses CSS transition which must be replaced with Lynx animation; safe-area-inset-bottom maps to Lynx safe area API; close-on-popstate not applicable in Lynx (no browser history); built-in icons (wechat, weibo, etc.) require bundled image assets since Lynx has no font icon CDN
Demo page path: src/pages/share-sheet/index.vue

acceptanceCriteria: Props: title (string, default '') — text content of the sidebar item; dot (boolean, default false) — shows a small red dot badge indicator; badge (number|string, default '') — badge count or text shown on the item; badge-props (BadgeProps, no default) — additional props passed through to the Badge component; disabled (boolean, default false) — disables the item, preventing selection and applying disabled styles; url (string, no default) — navigates to this URL when the item is clicked; to (string|object, no default) — Vue Router target route, equivalent to router-link 'to' prop; replace (boolean, default false) — when using 'to', replaces history entry instead of pushing
Events: click — emitted when the item is clicked, argument is index: number (the item's index within the Sidebar)
Slots: title — custom content for the item title area
Methods: none exposed via ref

Lynx adaptations: url and to navigation props are not applicable in Lynx (no browser router/href); replace prop is not applicable; badge rendering via van-badge must use Lynx-compatible badge component; dot indicator uses absolute positioning which must be verified in Lynx; active state styling (left border + background) must use Lynx-compatible style approach
Demo page path: src/pages/sidebar/index.vue

userStories.title: Implement Sticky with full Vant parity
userStories.description: Port the Vant Sticky component to Vue Lynx with full feature parity. All props, events, slots, and methods must match Vant's API.
acceptanceCriteria: Props: position (string, default: 'top', options: 'top'|'bottom') — which edge to stick to when scrolled out of view; offset-top (number|string, default: 0) — offset from top when position is 'top', supports px/vw/vh/rem units; offset-bottom (number|string, default: 0) — offset from bottom when position is 'bottom', supports px/vw/vh/rem units; z-index (number|string, default: 99) — z-index applied when sticky is active; container (Element, default: undefined) — container DOM element to constrain the sticky behavior within
Events: change — emitted when sticky state changes (becomes fixed or unfixed), arg: isFixed: boolean; scroll — emitted while scrolling, arg: { scrollTop: number, isFixed: boolean }
Slots: default — content to be made sticky
Methods: none defined

Lynx adaptations: CSS position:sticky is not available in Lynx — must implement sticky behavior using scroll event listeners and programmatic fixed positioning; scroll position must be tracked via Lynx scroll events (bindscroll on parent scroll container); element position must be measured using Lynx's createSelectorQuery or equivalent to get initial offset; fixed positioning in Lynx uses position:fixed with top/bottom set dynamically; container constraint (stopping scroll before container scrolls out) requires measuring container bounds and computing when sticky should 'un-fix'; offset-top/offset-bottom unit parsing (px/vw/vh/rem) must convert to Lynx pixel values; z-index must be applied via Lynx style; scroll and change events must fire with correct data as Lynx scroll fires

Methods: confirm () => void - stop scrolling and emit confirm event, getSelectedOptions () => PickerOption[] - get current selected options

userStories.title: Implement CountDown with full Vant parity

userStories.title: Implement RollingText with full Vant parity

userStories.title: Implement ImagePreview with full Vant parity
userStories.description: Port the Vant ImagePreview component to Vue Lynx with full feature parity. All props, events, slots, and methods must match Vant's API.
acceptanceCriteria: Props: v-model:show (boolean, default: false) — controls visibility of the ImagePreview; images (string[], default: []) — array of image URLs to display; start-position (number | string, default: 0) — initial image index when opened; swipe-duration (number | string, default: 300) — swipe animation duration in milliseconds; show-index (boolean, default: true) — whether to display the current index indicator; show-indicators (boolean, default: false) — whether to show swipe dot indicators; loop (boolean, default: true) — whether to enable looped swiping; double-scale (boolean, default: true) — whether double-tap zoom gesture is enabled; before-close ((action: number) => boolean | Promise<boolean>, no default) — callback called before closing, return false to prevent close; close-on-popstate (boolean, default: true) — whether to close when browser back button is pressed; close-on-click-image (boolean, default: true, since v4.8.3) — whether clicking the image closes the preview; close-on-click-overlay (boolean, default: true, since v4.6.4) — whether clicking the overlay closes the preview; vertical (boolean, default: false, since v4.8.6) — whether to use vertical swipe gesture; class-name (string | Array | object, no default) — custom class applied to the popup; max-zoom (number | string, default: 3) — maximum zoom scale; min-zoom (number | string, default: 1/3) — minimum zoom scale; closeable (boolean, default: false) — whether to show a close icon button; close-icon (string, default: 'clear') — icon name for the close button; close-icon-position (string, default: 'top-right') — position of close icon, possible values: 'top-left', 'top-right', 'bottom-left', 'bottom-right'; transition (string, default: 'van-fade') — transition animation name; overlay-class (string | Array | object, no default) — custom class applied to the overlay; overlay-style (object, no default) — custom inline styles for the overlay; teleport (string | Element, no default) — target element to mount the ImagePreview into

Events: close ({ index: number, url: string }) — emitted when ImagePreview is closing; closed (no arguments) — emitted after ImagePreview close animation completes; change (index: number) — emitted when the active image changes; scale ({ index: number, scale: number }) — emitted when the user zooms the current image; long-press ({ index: number }) — emitted when the user long-presses the current image

Slots: index ({ index: number }) — custom index indicator content; cover (no props) — custom content overlaid on the image preview; image ({ src: string, onLoad: Function, style: object }) — custom image rendering, used for video or other media
Methods (via ref): resetScale() — resets the current image's zoom to default (since v4.7.4); swipeTo(index: number, options?: SwipeToOptions) — programmatically swipe to a specific index
Function API: showImagePreview(images: string[] | ImagePreviewOptions) returns an ImagePreview instance with a close() method

Lynx adaptations: touch-based pinch-to-zoom must use Lynx gesture system; teleport and popstate APIs are not available in Lynx; overlay and transition must use Lynx equivalents; the image slot with video content is not applicable in Lynx; close-on-popstate has no Lynx equivalent and should be treated as no-op
Demo page path: packages/vant-lynx/src/demo/image-preview/index.vue

userStories.title: Implement Watermark with full Vant parity
userStories.description: Port the Vant Watermark component to Vue Lynx with full feature parity. All props, events, slots, and methods must match Vant's API.
acceptanceCriteria: Props: width (number, watermark tile width in px, default: 100); height (number, watermark tile height in px, default: 100); z-index (number|string, default: 100); content (string, text watermark content, default: -); image (string, image URL for watermark — takes priority over content when both provided, default: -); rotate (number|string, rotation angle in degrees, default: -22); full-page (boolean, whether watermark covers full screen, default: true); gap-x (number, horizontal gap between watermark tiles in px, default: 0); gap-y (number, vertical gap between watermark tiles in px, default: 0); text-color (string, color of text watermark, default: '#dcdee0'); opacity (number|string, opacity of the entire watermark layer, default: -)
Slots: content — custom HTML watermark content (inline styles only, no self-closing tags); takes priority over content prop and image prop
Methods: none exposed via ref

Lynx adaptations: Vant's web implementation renders watermarks onto an HTML5 Canvas element and sets it as a repeated CSS background-image — Canvas API is not available in Lynx; must implement watermark tile rendering using Lynx drawing primitives or by tiling image/text elements directly in the component tree; full-page mode must cover the Lynx page root equivalent; CSS background-repeat tiling pattern must be replicated with absolute-positioned repeated elements or a Lynx canvas alternative; HTML slot content (arbitrary inline HTML with mix-blend-mode etc.) cannot be rendered to canvas in Lynx — this slot may need to be marked as unsupported or partially supported; rotate transform must use Lynx transform style; opacity prop maps to Lynx opacity style
Demo page path: src/pages/watermark/index.vue
